title: cynometra vestita
intro: Cynometra vestita, also known as the Burmah ironwood or Burma iron tree, is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cynometra vestita include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to be consumed as a tea or used as a topical treatment for various ailments.
